title: Dendromyza trinervia
taxonomy: Dendromyza trinervia is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It is native to Africa. The classification of Dendromyza trinervia is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Santalales, family Santalaceae, genus Dendromyza, species D. trinervia. Further classification includes the classification of the order to be Santalales. Dendromyza trinervia is a type of mistletoe.

intro: Dendromyza trinervia is a plant species, also known as the three-veined Dendromyza or Three-veined Dendromyza, a medicinal plant native to tropical regions. The health benefits of Dendromyza trinervia include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dendromyza trinervia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine practices in various parts of the world, including Southeast Asia and Africa.
